landing.title: Better patient monitoring, built from the services already in this project.
landing.description: We now frame CareCaller like an actual operating dashboard: calmer information hierarchy, stronger state cues, and direct paths into the patient and clinician workflows that already exist in this app.

monitoring.title: Monitoring that feels alive
monitoring.description: Turn calls, medication context, and escalation logic into a patient state dashboard that is easy to trust at a glance.

corrections.title: Patient corrections stay in the loop
corrections.description: Medication fixes and care-team messages feed directly back into the next interaction without adding a new service layer.

clinician.title: Clinician view built for action
clinician.description: Escalations, recent calls, appointments, and patient messages sit together so a follow-up plan takes one pass instead of five.

landing.workflowDescription: No new backend dependencies, no fake demo data layer, and no separate dashboard engine. The UI now leans into the current CareCaller stack instead of hiding it behind generic cards.
landing.changesTitle: The dashboards now carry the experience.

changes.patient: Patient view emphasizes monitoring state, next steps, and safer self-service actions.
changes.clinician: Clinician view condenses the care context into a faster operations layout.
changes.ui: Shared UI now uses softer surfaces, stronger typography, and better mobile spacing.

dashboard.title: Your Care Summary
dashboard.emergency: If you feel unsafe, call 911 immediately
dashboard.staffWillContact: A staff member will contact you soon
dashboard.messagePlaceholder: Type a message for your care team...
dashboard.messageError: Failed to send. Please try again.
dashboard.headline: Patient state, without losing the care context.
dashboard.subtitle: This dashboard pulls calls, medications, messages, and upcoming appointments into a single view that feels much closer to CareFlow.
dashboard.noSymptoms: No active symptoms are currently logged.

high.title: High attention
high.summary: A clinician follow-up should happen soon. Keep an eye on symptoms, messages, and the next appointment window.

watch.title: Watch closely
watch.summary: The current state looks stable enough for home monitoring, but the care team should stay ready for a rapid adjustment.

steady.title: Steady monitoring
steady.summary: No urgent signal is active right now. The best next step is to keep the patient engaged and make the next touchpoint easy.

dashboard.responseWindowHint: Severity and escalation state drive how quickly the team should intervene.
dashboard.messagingFallback: Messaging is the current fallback path for updates.
dashboard.pendingEscalations: {count, plural, one {# pending escalation} other {# pending escalations}}
dashboard.costSupportEmpty: Savings programs will appear here when the timeline records assistance options for active medications.

clinician.title: Patient Timeline
clinician.subtitle: Patient context, recent activity, and the next care moves in a single working view.
clinician.noSymptoms: No open symptoms are currently recorded.
clinician.noMeds: No active medications are currently recorded.
clinician.noEscalations: No escalations are pending right now.

recommendedAction.urgent: Escalate to clinician review and confirm the follow-up window today.
recommendedAction.midWithAppointment: Keep the next appointment intact and review new patient messages before the visit.
recommendedAction.midWithoutAppointment: Schedule a near-term check-in and watch for any medication corrections or symptom drift.
recommendedAction.stable: Continue home monitoring and use the next outreach touchpoint to verify medication adherence.

clinician.noCompletedCalls: No completed calls have been logged for this patient yet.

scheduling.patientHeadline: Closest specialists for the patient’s active symptoms
scheduling.clinicianHeadline: Recommended follow-up slots, ranked by distance
scheduling.patientSubtitle: These recommendations are built from the patient’s current symptoms and the doctor schedule file you added. Each option is sorted by distance and next available opening.
scheduling.clinicianSubtitle: Use these symptom-matched calendars to turn the current state into a concrete follow-up plan. Booking here creates a normal appointment in the existing CareCaller flow.
scheduling.bookingFailed: Could not schedule that slot. Try another option.
scheduling.noRecommendations: No symptom-based appointment recommendations are available right now.

content.savingsFound: Savings found for {drug}: {count, plural, one {# option} other {# options}}
